summary: Comprehensive testing of Inventory Snapshot Export feature (iteration_67) - all tests passed. Backend: 25/25 pytest tests passed covering snapshot endpoint, export endpoint, and regression. Frontend: Export Snapshot button visible on Balances tab, properly opens snapshot export URL with JSON download. All existing tabs work correctly.

issues: Pre-existing React hydration warning: '<tr> cannot be a child of <span>' in table tbody - cosmetic, non-blocking (same as iteration_66)

Backend: Balance rows cleanly strip internal flags (is_short, is_low) and expose status field (OK/LOW/SHORT)

seed_data_used: Used existing Hormel Foods workspace (ID: 366bfcce-4d68-4daf-b9f4-cb05a35de8c8) with 29 unique items, 32 balance rows (24 OK, 3 LOW, 5 SHORT), 5 reorder recommendations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Snapshot Export feature fully tested. Endpoints: GET /api/inventory-ledger/snapshot (returns JSON), GET /api/inventory-ledger/snapshot/export (returns downloadable JSON file). Parameters: customer_id (required), item (optional filter), include_reorders (default true). Response structure: generated_at (ISO timestamp), context (customer_id, item_filter, include_reorders), summary (9 metrics matching dashboard-summary exactly), balances (clean rows with status field), reorders (optional array). Export adds Content-Disposition header with filename format snapshot_{name}_{timestamp}.json. Frontend: Export Snapshot button on Balances tab toolbar (data-testid='inv-export-snapshot-btn').

response_fields.balances: array of balance rows with status field

export_button.location: Balances tab toolbar (after Import CSV button)
export_button.action: Opens snapshot/export URL in new tab for download
